name: Mind the Gaps
description: Scans your library for gaps (missing movies in partially-owned collections and franchises, missing series content, unowned titles from actor and director filmographies, and related recommendations) using TMDB, Trakt, TheTVDB, and TVmaze, with music and book sources (MusicBrainz, OpenLibrary, Discogs). Presents an easy todo list for filling out your collection, diagnoses why a title is reported missing, surfaces where to watch each item, and can optionally mint native virtual placeholders for missing items.
overview: Find what's missing and related in your library and build a todo list to complete it.

versions.changelog: 10.11.4.0 - Discovery lists: complete or explore a curated TMDB list, MDBList community list, or Trakt list the way you complete a collection (a TMDB list takes a pasted themoviedb.org/list URL, a Trakt list a numeric id or slug), complete a Books subject from OpenLibrary, and try any source from a Run button without a full scan. Series completeness now follows your library's own providers, merging TheMovieDb, TheTVDB, and TVmaze season by season in your order and reconciling episodes by air date and folded title, so a renumbered or merged two-part episode reads as owned; the per-provider checkboxes are gone, and you can re-check one series without a rescan. Music and books are on by default, Creator works surfaces an owned person's missing series as well as films, and Discogs completes a label's releases and an owned artist's discography quickly and accurately. Diagnose and the identification audit cover albums and books, explain an episode-numbering mismatch, and export an AI-analysis dossier. Round out a gap with a personal TODO list, a send to Radarr, Sonarr, or Jellyseerr, or a reversible virtual placeholder for any domain. The report regroups its settings, labels every tab for the media type in view, enlarges titles, collapses sections, and keeps shareable links short, while scans shrug off a transient provider failure and never log an API key.

10.11.3.10 - Two new opt-in sources, each with its own settings. Complete a Books subject from OpenLibrary (for example every book tagged science fiction or fantasy) under Complete what you own, and discover the unowned movies and shows on a Trakt list under Discover new titles. The TMDB-list setting now accepts a pasted themoviedb.org/list URL, not just a bare id. In the Shows set-completion view each series shows its year in the header, so a reboot is told apart from the original (Quantum Leap 1989 versus 2022).

10.11.3.9 - Discogs discography scanning is faster and more accurate. It now checks only the artists your library has already tagged with a Discogs id, instead of guessing every artist by name (which was slow and sometimes matched the wrong act), and it fetches only album-level master releases, so a stray single or promo no longer turns up as a missing album.

10.11.3.8 - Series completeness is more accurate and now follows your library's own providers. Each series is checked against every metadata provider your Shows library uses (TheMovieDb, TheTVDB, TVmaze), merged season by season in your configured order so your preferred provider is the authority, a lower-ranked one can only add a season none above it lists, and your own missing-episode list is the last-chance fallback; a stale or wrong season no longer reports every episode as missing. Episodes are reconciled by air date and folded title, not just number, so a provider that renumbers, reorders, or merges a two-part episode no longer flags your owned episodes as missing. The separate per-provider cross-check checkboxes are gone (TheTVDB still needs its API key). You can re-check a single series for missing episodes without a full rescan. The identification audit and the Diagnose popup now cover music albums and books, not just movies and shows. Row titles are larger and each source's section can be collapsed.

10.11.3.7 - A transient provider failure mid-scan (a TMDB or music-source hiccup) no longer blanks a collection's or a discography's gaps from the report. A multi-episode file (S01E01-E02) is counted across its whole span, so its later parts no longer read as missing. A failed report load now shows an error instead of spinning forever, and no API key reaches the server log. Discogs fills the gaps in a MusicBrainz-covered artist's discography, surfacing the albums one provider lists and the other misses. The Diagnose popup verifies an episode by its number, and when the number is absent but you own the same title at another number (a two-part or off-by-one numbering mismatch) it points you to renumber the file rather than calling the episode missing.

10.11.3.6 - A personal todo list: an Add to my TODO action on any gap, a My TODO list modal that groups what you saved by domain and verifies each item against your library, and a Markdown checklist export. Minting now covers any domain, not just movies: a missing series, album, or book becomes a tagged, reversible virtual placeholder attached to its set, artist, or author. A long-running show you only partly own keeps its earlier and later missing seasons listed instead of hiding them as a same-named reboot. A new Detailed API logging toggle logs every external API request and response to the server log, for debugging a source or acquisition target that is not behaving.

10.11.3.5 - Creator works now surfaces an owned actor or director's missing series, not just their films, and the music and book sources are on by default. Settings are regrouped into Complete what you own and Discover new titles, and TMDB lists gain their own Scan TMDB lists toggle. Explore a source moved out of settings onto a button on the report, so trying a source no longer touches the settings form. The Diagnose popup gains Export for AI analysis, a Markdown dossier you can hand to any AI to work out why a title was not matched. A title that is on a curated TMDB or MDBList list and is also recommended now groups under the list in Discover. You can send a missing movie or series to Radarr, Sonarr, or Jellyseerr from the report. The MDBList search no longer fails on a stray character.

10.11.3.4 - Explore a source ad-hoc: a Run now beside each studio, keyword, Discogs label, TMDB list, and MDBList list picker finds its unowned titles and adds them straight to the report without a full scan, so you can try a source before committing to it; Clear explorations removes them again, and a later full scan drops any you did not save. Also fixes a provider link on a group header that also collapsed the group, and stops the browser from offering to save the API key fields as a login password.

10.11.3.3 - Fixes the Markdown export filename, which saved as mind-the-gaps-parts.md instead of being named for the domain and tab in view. Under the hood the report rendering was rebuilt onto a small set of shared element builders with consistent HTML escaping, and repeated inline styles moved into CSS classes so a custom theme has cleaner hooks; the report itself looks and behaves the same.

10.11.3.2 - MDBList community lists and TMDB lists join as discovery sources, so you can complete or explore a curated list the way you complete a collection. The Discover tab now groups its results by source, collapsing each list or recommending title under its own name to match the Markdown export. Tab and group labels follow the media type in view (Series completion for shows, Discography and Artist works for music, Author works for books, and Discover for recommendations), and shareable view links now stay within URL length limits.

10.11.3.0 - A new Discogs source completes curated record labels and an owned artist's discography (needs a Discogs token), chosen with the same type-ahead chip picker as studios and keywords. Diagnose goes deeper and wider: a networked "Deeper analysis" compares external ids to tell a real misidentification from a coincidental title clash, episode and season gaps get their own diagnosis that spots a same-named reboot mis-tagged onto a series (the V 1984 versus V 2009 case), and album and book gaps are diagnosable too. The report now links out everywhere: music rows link to MusicBrainz and Discogs, books to OpenLibrary, the Diagnose ids are clickable, and each creator or set group header links the source itself (an author's OpenLibrary page, an actor or director's TMDB page, an artist's MusicBrainz or Discogs page, a studio, keyword, label, or collection page); every link carries a per-provider CSS class, a data-provider attribute, and a title and aria-label, so a theme can inject service icons (the README has a ready-to-paste snippet under Advanced CSS customization). Under the hood, every external call now sits behind a read-through cache with proactive per-service rate pacing (MusicBrainz and Discogs) and a circuit breaker that moves the scan past a down service, the scan runs its sources concurrently and streams results into the report per item so a slow provider no longer holds up the others, and stable id-to-name lookups are cached for far longer. The library no longer reports a reboot's later seasons as missing episodes of the single-season original, a Name-plus- year match now prefers an exact-year hit over the one-year tolerance, and the Books (OpenLibrary) source is hardened against real-world author data. In settings, the Discogs toggle and token sit in the Data sources section, and Markdown exports are named by domain so different tabs no longer overwrite each other.

10.11.2.2 (beta) - Every gap now links out to its providers. Music rows link to MusicBrainz and Discogs, book rows to OpenLibrary, and each creator or set group header links the source itself: an author's OpenLibrary page, an actor or director's TMDB page, an artist's MusicBrainz or Discogs page, and a studio, keyword, label, or collection page, so you can open the creator or set, not just its missing items. The Diagnose popup's MusicBrainz, Discogs, and OpenLibrary ids are clickable just like the TMDB, IMDb, and TheTVDB ones. For theming, every provider link carries a per-provider CSS class (cgProvider-tmdb, cgProvider-discogs, and so on) and a data-provider attribute, so a stylesheet can inject service icons (see the README's Advanced CSS customization section). In settings, the Discogs toggle and token move into the Data sources section with the other providers, and the Webhook URL moves to the bottom of it. The Markdown export filename now includes the domain, so Movies and Music exports of the same tab no longer overwrite each other.

10.11.2.1 (beta) - A new Discogs source completes curated record labels and an owned artist's discography (needs a Discogs token), chosen with the same type-ahead chip picker as studios and keywords. Diagnose goes deeper and wider: a networked "Deeper analysis" compares external ids to tell a real misidentification from a coincidental title clash, episode and season gaps now have their own diagnosis that spots a same-named reboot mis-tagged onto a series (the V 1984 versus V 2009 case), and album and book gaps are diagnosable too. The library no longer reports a reboot's later seasons as missing episodes of the single-season original, and a Name-plus-year match now prefers an exact-year hit over the one-year tolerance. Under the hood every external call now sits behind a read-through cache with proactive per-service rate pacing (MusicBrainz and Discogs), a circuit breaker that moves the scan past a down service, and longer caching of stable id-to-name lookups; the scan runs its sources concurrently and streams results into the report per item, so a slow provider no longer holds up the others and gaps appear as they are found. The Books (OpenLibrary) source is hardened against real-world author data. The dashboard is now authored as separate report, settings, CSS, and JS files and concatenated at build time (no visible change).

10.11.2.0 - The first public release since 10.11.1.0, rolling up the 10.11.1.x betas. The headline is Diagnose: a per-gap popup explains why something is reported missing, laying the gap and the owned items that look like it side by side and leading with a plain verdict (owned under the wrong id, an owned item already holds this id, stale, a wrong kind of id, or genuinely missing); a "Deeper analysis" button confirms against the source provider, and a library-wide identification audit runs the same check and downloads as Markdown. Diagnosis weighs both title and year, so a remake that shares a name with something you own (the 1960 Ocean's Eleven against the 2001 remake) is no longer reported as that title under the wrong id. Curated studio and keyword sets are now chosen with a type-ahead chip picker (search TheMovieDb, pick a match, it becomes a removable chip), keyboard and screen-reader accessible, and the Set completion tab groups by kind of set (collections and franchises, studios, keywords, series, and discographies). The Markdown exports gained structure: grouped sections, a table of contents, and native collapsible blocks (closed by default) for a large export. Also: a JustWatch search button on rows that lack a JustWatch link; titles that begin with "The" file under both T and the next word's letter; provider overviews are stripped of stray HTML and episode and season rows gained their search and open icons; a fresh install opens to settings until the first scan; and the settings panel warns about unsaved changes. Breaking change: the old free-text studio-names field was removed in favor of the chip picker, so any studios configured by name (rather than id) are dropped and must be re-picked.

10.11.1.3 (pre-release) - Curated sets get a chip picker, exports collapse. Studios and keywords are now chosen with a type-ahead chip picker: search TheMovieDb, pick a match, and it becomes a removable chip, so you never touch a numeric id; the picker is keyboard and screen-reader accessible. Breaking change: the old free-text studio-names field is gone, so any studios configured by name (rather than id) are dropped and must be re-picked. The Markdown exports gained native collapsible sections (closed by default) so a large export opens to a scannable list: the gap export lists each individual studio, keyword, or collection on its own, and the identification audit makes each finding and each duplicate-id group its own collapsible. The audit's open-in-Jellyfin links were also fixed to render correctly.

10.11.1.2 (pre-release) - Sharper diagnosis, easier exports. The Diagnose action now weighs both title and year, so a remake that shares a name with something you own (the 1960 Ocean's Eleven against the 2001 remake, the original Home Alone against the 2023 reboot) is no longer reported as that owned title under the wrong id; a year more than one apart is treated as a different release, while a missing year still falls back to the title match. The Markdown exports gained structure and a table of contents: the gap export groups each domain by its kind of source (collections and franchises, studios, keywords, series, and discographies), and the identification audit groups its findings by reason, each with contents links to jump between sections. Titles that begin with "The" now file under both T and the next word's letter, so "The Highlander" is also found under H. Curated keyword sets show the keyword's name instead of its numeric id.

10.11.1.1 (pre-release) - Diagnose, deeper. A new Diagnose action explains why a gap is reported missing: a per-gap popup lays the gap and the owned items that look like it side by side, and leads with a plain verdict (owned under the wrong id, an owned item already holds this id, the gap looks stale, a wrong kind of id, or genuinely missing). It corroborates by IMDb and TheTVDB id, so it still catches a title you own under a localized name, and a "Deeper analysis" button confirms against the source provider and fills in missing ids. A library-wide identification audit runs the same check across the whole library and downloads as Markdown, with a link on each finding back into Jellyfin. Also in this build: a JustWatch search button on movie and show rows that lack a JustWatch link (using your configured region); the Set completion tab now groups by kind of set (collections and franchises, studios, keywords, and music discographies); provider overviews are stripped of stray HTML and episode and season rows gained their missing search and open icons; a fresh install opens to settings until the first scan; and the settings panel warns about unsaved changes. This is the first build on the new beta release channel.

10.11.1.0 - Initial release. Mind the Gaps scans your library and builds a dashboard todo list of what is missing or worth adding: movies missing from a collection or franchise; missing seasons and episodes (optionally cross-checked against TVmaze and TheTVDB); films from an owned actor or director's filmography (with an independent Trakt cross-check); curated studio and keyword sets; and TMDB recommendations. Experimental music and books sources are included too. It shows where to watch each gap, links out to TMDB, IMDb, TheTVDB, and JustWatch, and can optionally mint reversible virtual placeholders for missing collection movies.

10.11.0.16 - Sturdier scans against flaky APIs. Every external service call now retries rate-limit and transient failures (honouring Retry-After) instead of dropping the lookup, a service that keeps failing is set aside for a couple of minutes so the scan moves on to the others rather than stalling, the scan saves its progress to disk as it goes so a crash or restart no longer loses the batch, and every request now identifies the plugin (with its version) to the API.
10.11.0.15 - Fixes the plugin failing to load. The package now also ships TMDbLib and Newtonsoft.Json.

10.11.0.14 (pre-release) - One page, faster big tabs, and self-updating "where to watch". Settings now live on the report page: the gear opens an inline settings panel (accordion) and closes the report, so the plugin has a single sidebar entry. The A-Z bar is now a selector that renders one letter group at a time (with a leading "*" for all), on every tab including Set completion, and Creator works builds each group's rows only when you expand it, so a tab with tens of thousands of rows opens quickly. A small green dot marks a collapsed group that has a streaming source. Music gaps split by how you own the artist: an album artist you collect yields a Set-completion discography, an artist you only own a track by yields a Creator-works "artist works" list. "Where to watch" stays current on its own: a new "Refresh where to watch" scheduled task drains the whole backlog, a looked-up result is served instantly while a refresh runs behind the scenes, and the cache lifetime is settable (default 24 hours). Fix: a music set's search button now searches music instead of box sets.

10.11.0.13 - Report polish, accessibility, and a settings-link fix. The plugin's "Settings" button on the Plugins page now opens the configuration page instead of the report, and the report shows the installed plugin version next to the gear. Group headers use compact icons for resolve and not-interested, ordered search, open in Jellyfin, resolve, not-interested; a collapsed entry shows just the coverage percent (the full count on hover). The Markdown export now includes a search link for each gap. With one media domain shown at a time, the redundant top-level Movies/Shows group is gone and the summary line reads a little larger. Every icon control now has an accessible label so a screen reader announces the action. Fixes: the Type filter stacking onto two lines, and collapsed grid cells reflowing on hover.

10.11.0.12 - A relevance gate keeps Creator works and Recommendations actionable on a large library: a TMDB vote floor (plus an optional cast-billing limit) drops obscure and bit-part credits, all tunable in settings. Set completion now lays its collapsed series and collections out in responsive columns instead of one very tall list, and a magnifying-glass icon opens a scoped Jellyfin search for any title, series, collection, or creator. The Type filter now lists the media domains actually present in each tab (so Music and Books gaps are reachable) and is a one-domain-at-a-time selector. You can resolve or mark "not interested" every episode under a series or season in one batch. The Markdown export folds the domain into the heading with a linked "Watch" and a link back to the same view. Fixes: the stale-list "Rescan now" button gave no feedback, and some season links were missing. Background runners now claim their run flag without locking the status counters.

10.11.0.11 - Faster loading, broader coverage, and hardening ahead of 1.0. The report now loads one tab at a time, so a large library opens quickly. Filmography, recommendations, and the TVmaze/TheTVDB series cross-checks rotate through your library over successive scans, so coverage builds up instead of re-checking the same first items every run; stale entries are pruned automatically and a "Reset scan rotation" button forces a fresh cycle. The "Look up where to watch" button shows how many titles remain, live progress while running, and when the backlog is cleared. Empty tabs now explain which filter to relax, search also matches the creator or recommending title, group headers are keyboard and screen-reader friendly, and the settings page shows a quick gap summary. You can copy a link that reopens the exact tab and filters in another browser or for someone else. API keys are masked with a reveal toggle. Under the hood, minting works from gap ids, report reads are snapshotted against the background pass, background tasks stop cleanly on shutdown, and links are limited to http(s).

10.11.0.10 - Reliability and polish ahead of 1.0. The scan and the background "where to watch" pass no longer overwrite each other, so results from a scan that finishes mid-pass are kept. Tab counts now match the filtered list, and "Hide items with no sources" hides only titles already looked up and found empty, not ones not yet checked. Your scroll position, expanded groups, and selection are kept when a row is resolved or a filter changes. The provider filter is populated from the saved report and gains enable-all and disable-all controls. Concurrent minting can no longer create duplicate collections.

10.11.0.9 - Resolve a gap you don't consider missing (for example two listed episodes that are one combined file): mark it with a note and it drops off the list. A "Show resolved" filter brings resolved rows back, greyed, with their note and a Clear button; resolutions persist across rescans. New "Hide upcoming" filter for gaps with a future release date. The Settings button on the plugin page now opens the settings, not the report. The "open in Jellyfin" icon now sits next to each row's title.

10.11.0.8 - Missing-episode rows now show where to watch the show. Availability look-ups are grouped by title (episodes of a series share one call) and remember when a title has no sources, showing "No streaming sources found" instead of a look-up that comes back empty. The report is version-stamped and nudges you to rescan after a plugin upgrade (rebuilds stale links). Fixed group headers not toggling after navigating away and back. Creator Works and Recommendations letter groups start collapsed, plus a back-to-top button. The watch-page link is now "Watch", next to "Where to watch:". Settings page reorganized into labelled sections.

10.11.0.7 - Availability now runs as a background "Look up where to watch" pass instead of slowing the scan. Rows link to IMDb/TheTVDB, not just TMDB; fixed TheTVDB episode/season 404s. New "Open in Jellyfin" icons (show/season/item, new tab). Recommendations show the recommending title's year and kind and link to it. Creator Works and Recommendations now group alphabetically (Unicode-aware). The report is saved atomically.

10.11.0.5 - Add report filters: a "where to watch" type filter (subscription, free, ads, rent, buy; flatrate now shows as Subscription) and a "hide specials" toggle that also drops shows left with no other gaps. Filter selections are remembered per browser.

10.11.0.4 - Fix the report styling (collapsible groups, carets, and buttons were dead because the stylesheet sat in the page head, which Jellyfin drops). Split the report into one tab per axis (Set completion, Creator works, Recommendations). Group show episodes by season (season 0 is Specials). Click a row to reveal its overview.

10.11.0.3 - Split media domains into Movies and Shows (was one Video bucket). The dashboard now groups gaps in a collapsible Pattern > Movies/Shows > source tree, with link buttons and clickable JustWatch availability. Add a Refresh button (reload saved results without re-scanning) and finer-grained scan progress.

10.11.0.2 - Run scans in the background so large libraries no longer time out the request; the dashboard polls for progress. Make the per-show missing-episode cap configurable (0 lists them all). Clearer ownership-index logging.

10.11.0.1 - Fix mint patterns not persisting (minting did nothing) and the report page being unreachable (page-name case collision). Add a per-row Mint button, dry-run previews, elapsed-time logging, and clickable key-signup links on the settings page.
10.11.0.0 - Initial release: gap scanning across collections, filmographies, series content, and recommendations, with a dashboard todo list and experimental virtual placeholders.
